tasks.title: Honor context cancellation during backoff/poll sleeps
evidence: rg -n "time.Sleep(" internal/fetch internal/queue/redis.go -> internal/fetch/playwright_fetcher.go:596 time.Sleep(time.Duration(pollMs) * time.Millisecond)
plan: Introduce a small helper (e.g., sleepWithContext) in an appropriate internal package to block until either the timer fires or ctx is canceled.
Replace the production time.Sleep calls in chromedp_fetcher.go, playwright_fetcher.go, and queue/redis.go with the context-aware helper and propagate ctx cancellation where needed.
Add focused tests that assert cancellation returns quickly instead of waiting the full delay.
Verify by running go test ./internal/fetch ./internal/queue and confirming tests pass and cancellation-aware behavior is covered.
notes: Principles violated: F (Fail fast appropriately), H (Operational hygiene).
Why it matters: context cancellation (shutdowns, timeouts, request aborts) is delayed by hard sleeps, which can stall workers and make shutdowns slow or flaky.
Minimal fix: replace blocking sleeps with a ctx-aware sleep helper and cover cancellation in tests.

tasks.title: Remove !important from Joyride styles to satisfy Biome lint
scope: cd web && pnpm exec biome lint .
evidence: make ci -> biome lint warnings: web/src/index.css:1880, 1881, 1882, 1883, 1888, 1893, 1894 lint/complexity/noImportantStyles
plan: Adjust the Joyride selectors or load order to avoid !important (e.g., increase specificity with scoped selectors or move overrides after vendor styles).
Confirm visual parity for onboarding tooltip/spotlight styling.
Verify by running cd web && pnpm exec biome lint . and ensuring the noImportantStyles warnings are gone.
notes: Principles violated: G (Least astonishment), H (Operational hygiene).
Why it matters: lint warnings make CI noisy and normalize ignored style violations, which hides real regressions.
Minimal fix: remove !important by increasing selector specificity or adjusting style import order.

tasks.title: Reduce web build chunk size warning (>500 kB) via code-splitting
evidence: make ci -> pnpm run build warns: 'Some chunks are larger than 500 kB after minification' and suggests dynamic import/manualChunks
plan: Identify the largest chunk contributors (e.g., route-level or feature-level bundles) and choose a minimal split target.
Introduce dynamic imports or rollup manualChunks configuration to split the large bundle without changing runtime behavior.
Verify by running cd web && pnpm run build and confirming the chunk-size warning is gone or below the configured threshold.
notes: Principles violated: H (Operational hygiene), E (Make bugs hard to write) via performance regressions that are harder to notice.
Why it matters: oversized bundles slow initial load and can regress UX on constrained devices.
Minimal fix: split the largest routes/components into lazy-loaded chunks.

tasks.title: Split internal/cli/batch/batch.go (1237 LOC) into cohesive files
plan: Identify logical responsibility seams (config parsing, job setup, execution, reporting) and extract into new files within internal/cli/batch.
Keep public API unchanged; ensure helper functions remain package-private.
Verify by running go test ./internal/cli/batch and make test-ci.
notes: Principles violated: D (Localize change/SoC), A (KISS).
Why it matters: large files make change review error-prone and increase coupling between unrelated CLI behaviors.
Minimal fix: extract cohesive groups into smaller files without changing behavior.

tasks.title: Split web/src/App.tsx (1081 LOC) into smaller components
plan: Extract layout, provider wiring, and route composition into dedicated components/modules under web/src.
Keep routing behavior stable and reuse existing tests.
Verify by running cd web && pnpm run test and cd web && pnpm run build.
notes: Principles violated: D (Localize change/SoC), A (KISS).
Why it matters: oversized root components increase merge conflicts and make routing changes risky.
Minimal fix: split into smaller components without changing runtime behavior.

tasks.title: Split web/src/components/WatchManager.tsx (1037 LOC)
plan: Extract repeated UI sections and data hooks into subcomponents/hooks under web/src/components/watch/ (or similar).
Ensure props and state boundaries remain clear and reduce file size below 1000 LOC.
Verify by running cd web && pnpm run test and cd web && pnpm run build.
notes: Principles violated: D (Localize change/SoC), A (KISS).
Why it matters: large UI modules are difficult to test and review, increasing regression risk.
Minimal fix: split into cohesive subcomponents and hooks without altering behavior.

tasks.title: Split internal/distributed/redis_lock_test.go (1485 LOC) into focused test files
plan: Group tests by behavior (acquire/release, renew, contention, expiry) and move each group into its own *_test.go file.
Keep helper functions in a shared testutil file where appropriate.
Verify by running go test ./internal/distributed.
notes: Principles violated: D (Localize change/SoC), A (KISS).
Why it matters: extremely large test files make it hard to isolate failures and add new cases safely.
Minimal fix: split tests into coherent files without changing assertions.

tasks.title: Modularize web/src/index.css (2771 LOC) into smaller CSS files
plan: Split index.css into themed sections (base, layout, components, utilities) and import them in a stable order.
Preserve selector specificity and avoid visual regressions.
Verify by running cd web && pnpm run build and spot-checking key screens.
notes: Principles violated: D (Localize change/SoC), A (KISS).
Why it matters: monolithic CSS makes scope and overrides hard to reason about, increasing styling regressions.
Minimal fix: split CSS into smaller files with clear boundaries while keeping the cascade order.

tasks.title: Split internal/crawl/crawl.go (743 LOC) into cohesive files
plan: Extract crawler orchestration, worker logic, and utility helpers into separate files within internal/crawl.
Keep exported types/functions unchanged unless strictly necessary.
Verify by running go test ./internal/crawl.
notes: Principles violated: D (Localize change/SoC), A (KISS).
Why it matters: larger files increase coupling between crawler stages and make targeted changes risky.
Minimal fix: split by responsibility without behavior changes.

tasks.title: Split internal/store/store_retention_test.go (901 LOC) into focused test files
plan: Group retention tests by feature (jobs, crawl states, artifacts) and split into multiple *_test.go files.
Extract shared helpers into a testutil file if needed.
Verify by running go test ./internal/store.
notes: Principles violated: D (Localize change/SoC), A (KISS).
Why it matters: oversized test files are difficult to maintain and make failures harder to triage.
Minimal fix: split by responsibility while keeping assertions intact.

tasks.title: Split internal/webhook/dispatcher_test.go (803 LOC) into focused test files
plan: Separate dispatcher tests by scenario (success, retries, failures, rate limits) into multiple files.
Keep shared fixtures in a local test helper file.
Verify by running go test ./internal/webhook.
notes: Principles violated: D (Localize change/SoC), A (KISS).
Why it matters: large test files increase cognitive load and complicate adding coverage.
Minimal fix: split tests into cohesive files without altering behavior.
